title: Technical Engineer (Railway Power System)
description:
About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Technical Engineer to support our metro railway power system projects in Singapore.

This role focuses on design coordination, drawing and document review, interface management, and technical support to the project team, ensuring all engineering deliverables and submissions meet project, authority, and safety requirements.

The position does not require drafting. The ability to read, review, and interpret drawings and technical documents is sufficient.

Key Responsibilities

Design & Technical Coordination

  • Coordinate and support technical and design matters for metro railway power system projects
  • Review electrical drawings, schematics, design reports, and technical submissions
  • Ensure design deliverables comply with project requirements, specifications, and standards


Interface & Stakeholder Management

  • Coordinate interfaces between contractors, consultants, and system disciplines
  • Liaise with clients, consultants, and relevant authorities on design-related issues
  • Support resolution of technical queries, comments, and design clarifications


Documentation & Submission Control

  • Track design revisions, approvals, and technical submissions
  • Maintain proper records of engineering documentation and design coordination items
  • Prepare technical reports, meeting minutes, and coordination records


Testing & Commissioning Support

  • Support testing and commissioning activities for electrical and power systems
  • Provide technical assistance and problem-solving support during site and project execution


Compliance & Safety

  • Ensure engineering works comply with safety standards, statutory regulations, and project specifications


Requirements

  • Diploma or Degree in Electrical Engineering, Power Engineering, or related discipline
  • Minimum 3–5 years’ experience in metro, railway, LTA, or large-scale electrical infrastructure projects (required)
  • Good understanding of power distribution and/or traction power systems
  • Ability to read and interpret electrical drawings and technical documentation
  • Experience in design coordination or interface management is an advantage
  • AutoCAD knowledge is beneficial but not mandatory
  • Good communication, coordination, and stakeholder management skills
  • Independent, responsible, and able to work in a fast-paced project environment
  • Singaporean / WP / EP candidates are welcome


What We Offer

  • Competitive and negotiable salary package
  • Exposure to major national railway and power system infrastructure projects
  • Structured career development and long-term growth opportunities
  • Professional and collaborative work environment focused on safety and engineering excellence


About Us

China Railway Electrification Engineering Group Co., Ltd. (Singapore Branch) is a subsidiary of China Railway Group Limited (CREC), a Fortune Global 500 company.

Established in 2018, the Singapore Branch focuses on electrical and power engineering works for major metro and infrastructure projects across the region.

With a proud heritage dating back to 1958, when CREEG constructed China’s first-ever electrified railway, the Group has grown into a comprehensive “10-in-1” enterprise, spanning engineering construction, design, R&D, industrial manufacturing, testing, supervision, logistics, operation & maintenance, real estate, and investment.

We are committed to delivering projects that meet the highest international standards in safety, quality, and innovation.
